title: viola palustris subsp. herminii
intro: Viola palustris subsp. herminii, also known as marsh pansy or marsh violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, calming digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Viola palustris subsp. herminii include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
